message:
fix(ft-nn): Bilinear composes through outer + reshape + matmul [bd-yn0i]

ft-nn Bilinear's forward used to extract x1/x2/weight values,
compute y[b, k] = sum_{i,j} x1[b,i] W[k,i,j] x2[b,j] manually in
plain f64, and rebuild a non-grad leaf. The optional bias add was
autograd-aware but the prior leaf had already severed the tape —
gradient never flowed to x1, x2, or W.

Compose through autograd-aware primitives via vectorized outer
product + matmul:
   x1_b = unsqueeze(x1, 2) → expand to [batch, in1, in2]
   x2_b = unsqueeze(x2, 1) → expand to [batch, in1, in2]
   outer = x1_b * x2_b
   flat = reshape(outer, [batch, in1*in2])
   W_flat_t = transpose(reshape(W, [out, in1*in2]), 0, 1)
   y = matmul(flat, W_flat_t)              [batch, out]
   reshape to original output shape, then add bias as before.

Same bilinear math (y_k = x1^T W_k x2 + b_k) but the autograd
tape is preserved through x1, x2, W, and bias.

Closes frankentorch-yn0i. ft-nn lib tests: 517 passing.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -218,6 +218,7 @@
 {"id":"frankentorch-xsp","title":"Investigate beads/frankensqlite page-reuse integrity warning after writes","description":"## Context
`frankentorch-c84` diagnosed the residual `br doctor` / `PRAGMA integrity_check` warning in the FrankenTorch tracker.

## Findings
- live `.beads/beads.db` remained operational and in sync with `.beads/issues.jsonl`
- an isolated rebuild from canonical JSONL (`.beads/beads.db.rebuild_c84_20260407T013146Z`) reproduced the `Page N: never used` integrity warning
- a `VACUUM INTO` rewrite (`.beads/beads.db.vacuum_c84_20260407T013244Z`) passed `integrity_check`, `quick_check`, and `br doctor --db ...`
- after promoting the vacuumed copy to canonical `.beads/beads.db`, subsequent normal `br` operations reintroduced the warning (`page 19 is never used`)

## Interpretation
This looks like a `beads_rust` / frankensqlite writer bug or page-accounting bug, not JSONL drift and not unrecoverable logical data corruption.

## Artifacts
- `artifacts/beads_recovery/c84_snapshot_20260407T013033Z/`
- `.beads/beads.db.rebuild_c84_20260407T013146Z`
- `.beads/beads.db.vacuum_c84_20260407T013244Z`
- `.beads/beads.db.bad_20260407T013409Z`
- `artifacts/beads_recovery/c84_checkpoint_test_20260407T013229Z.db`

## Next step
Trace which write path (export, write_probe, cache rebuild, or WAL handling) is reintroducing unreachable pages after a clean vacuum.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-07T01:35:07.977449573Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-07T03:04:23.065716327Z","closed_at":"2026-04-07T03:04:23.065440955Z","close_reason":"Investigated and isolated to import-only / auto-import blocked_issues_cache rebuild path; follow-up tracked separately","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"frankentorch-xt9","title":"Add complex tensor real/imag/conj session ops","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-03-31T17:42:34.746744038Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-31T17:51:21.853786071Z","closed_at":"2026-03-31T17:51:21.853530317Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"frankentorch-xyq","title":"Fail closed on non-finite histc inputs and bounds","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-03-30T00:04:04.717732283Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-30T00:06:15.928299429Z","closed_at":"2026-03-30T00:06:15.928123987Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
+{"id":"frankentorch-yn0i","title":"ft-nn Bilinear severs autograd; composable through outer + reshape + matmul","description":"Bilinear at lib.rs:987 (forward_bilinear) computes y[b, k] = sum_{i,j} x1[b, i] * W[k, i, j] * x2[b, j] manually in plain f64, then rebuilds requires_grad=false leaf and adds optional bias on top. Severs autograd through x1/x2/W.

Composable via outer-product + matmul:
   x1_3d = unsqueeze(x1, 2)             # [batch, in1, 1]
   x2_3d = unsqueeze(x2, 1)             # [batch, 1, in2]
   outer = x1_3d * x2_3d                # [batch, in1, in2]
   flat  = reshape(outer, [batch, in1*in2])
   W_flat_t = transpose(reshape(W, [out, in1*in2]), 0, 1)
   y = matmul(flat, W_flat_t)           # [batch, out]
   reshape to original output shape, then add bias.

All ops autograd-aware so gradients flow to x1 / x2 / W / bias.","notes":"Fixed in this commit. Composed Bilinear through reshape + unsqueeze + expand + mul + reshape + transpose + matmul + add (bias). The bilinear formula y_k = x1^T W_k x2 + b_k is reformulated as y = vec(x1 outer x2) @ vec(W).T + b, which composes through autograd-aware primitives. ft-nn lib tests: 517.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-30T23:34:42.368655830Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-30T23:36:30.619298106Z","closed_at":"2026-04-30T23:36:30.616891679Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"frankentorch-ys3y","title":"tensor_kron generic path allocates a Vec per inner iteration (perf regression)","description":"fa1cf3d generalized tensor_kron to arbitrary ranks but the new generic path calls decode_coords inside the doubly-nested loop over a_vals x b_vals, allocating a fresh Vec<usize> of length rank for EACH iteration. For a 2D x 2D kron of 100x100 inputs that is 100M Vec allocations replacing what was a constant-time inner loop in the previous specialized 2D path.

Fix sketch: precompute strides for a_aligned, b_aligned, and out_shape ONCE before the loop. Inside the inner loop iterate over a_linear and b_linear directly, accumulating out_linear via stride math without intermediate coordinate Vecs. The stride for out at dim d is product(out_shape[d+1..]); a_aligned coordinate d contributes a_coord*b_aligned[d]*stride_out[d]+b_coord*stride_out[d] which can be precomputed as a per-(a_linear,b_linear) table.

Severity: MEDIUM. Functionality is correct; this is a hot-loop allocation regression that did not exist in the prior specialized 2D path. Stalls a primary tensor algebra op for any non-trivial input size.

Reproduction: cargo bench (or a hand-rolled timing harness) on tensor_kron of two 64x64 inputs vs the pre-fa1cf3d code.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-28T21:50:01.464788698Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-28T22:39:22.848622098Z","closed_at":"2026-04-28T22:39:22.845924321Z","close_reason":"Hoisted decode_coords Vec allocation out of the doubly-nested kron loop by precomputing a_strides and b_strides via contiguous_strides() ONCE before the outer loop. Per-iteration heap allocation eliminated; output coordinates derived in-place from a_linear/b_linear via integer division/modulo. Existing 5 kron tests still pass; new kron_4d_matches_explicit_index_decomposition regression added to lock the stride-based output layout against future drift. ft-api 1087 tests pass; clippy + ubs clean.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"frankentorch-ytlg","title":"tensor_inner is severed-autograd: composable through dot/matmul","description":"tensor_inner at lib.rs:15480 contracts the last dim of two tensors:
   c[..a, j_a, ..b, j_b] = sum_l a[..a, l] * b[..b, l]
where a has shape [a_outer..., a_last] and b has shape [b_outer..., b_last] with a_last == b_last. The forward extracts values, computes the contraction in plain f64, and rebuilds a requires_grad=false leaf. PyTorch's torch.inner is differentiable.

Compose through autograd primitives:
- 1-D case: delegate to tensor_dot (already autograd-aware).
- Higher-D: reshape both inputs to 2-D (a_outer, a_last) and (b_outer, b_last), compute matmul(a_flat, b_flat.T), reshape to the inner output shape. matmul + transpose + reshape are all autograd-aware.

Surfaced during /reality-check pass continuing from sirh fix.","notes":"Fixed in this commit. Composed tensor_inner through autograd primitives:
  - 1-D × 1-D: delegates to tensor_dot (autograd-aware via tape).
  - Higher-D: reshape both inputs to 2-D (outer, last), transpose b, matmul, reshape to inner output shape. matmul + transpose + reshape are all autograd-aware.
The output shape contract ([1] for 1-D inputs that previously returned shape [1]) is preserved via tensor_unsqueeze on the 0-D scalar tensor_dot returns. Added inner_1d_propagates_gradient_via_dot regression test ([1,2,3] · [4,5,6] = 32 → grad_a = b, grad_b = a). ft-api lib tests: 1150.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-30T21:02:14.070109061Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-30T21:04:29.737047942Z","closed_at":"2026-04-30T21:04:29.734574007Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"frankentorch-yur","title":"Standardize remaining top-level oracle mirror wording in analysis docs","description":"Update the non-historical top-level oracle mirror wording in EXISTING_PYTORCH_STRUCTURE.md and EXHAUSTIVE_LEGACY_ANALYSIS.md to use repo-relative `legacy_pytorch_code/pytorch` phrasing, while leaving the historical measured-from absolute path untouched.","notes":"Standardized the non-historical top-level oracle mirror wording in EXISTING_PYTORCH_STRUCTURE.md and EXHAUSTIVE_LEGACY_ANALYSIS.md to the same repo-relative `legacy_pytorch_code/pytorch` phrasing used by the other authoritative docs, while leaving the historical measured-from path untouched.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"docs","created_at":"2026-03-28T05:46:02.668383090Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-28T05:46:29.975320879Z","closed_at":"2026-03-28T05:46:29.974705662Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
